column1: # Detection Tuning Log
# Description: Records false positive investigations and tuning
#   actions for all OT detection rules. Serves as institutional
#   memory across shift changes and prevents repeated investigation
#   of known false positive patterns.
# Usage: Log every false positive investigation. Review during
#   quarterly lifecycle reviews. Track tuning effectiveness over time.
# Fields:
# EntryID           : string  — Sequential identifier (e.g.
# RuleID            : string  — Detection use case ID (e.g.
# RuleTitle         : string  — Detection use case title
# DateObserved      : datetime — When the false positive was observed
# AnalystName       : string  — Analyst who investigated
# FPDescription     : string  — Description of the false positive alert
# AffectedAsset     : string  — Asset(s) involved
# PurdueLevel       : string  — Purdue level of affected asset
# CrownJewelTier    : string  — Crown jewel tier of affected asset
# RootCause         : string  — Why the rule fired on legitimate activity
# RootCauseCategory : string  — Categorisation (see categories below)
# TuningAction      : string  — What was done to address the false positive
# TuningActionType  : string  — Category of tuning action (see types below)
# ActionDate        : datetime — When the tuning action was applied
# ActionBy          : string  — Who applied the tuning action
# EffectiveResult   : string  — Did the tuning resolve the FP? (Resolved/Partial/Unresolved)
# EscalatedToEng    : boolean — Was this FP escalated to OT engineering?
# EngFeedback       : string  — Engineering team's feedback (if escalated)
# Notes             : string  — Additional context
# Root Cause Categories:
#   - Maintenance Not Documented (maintenance window not in watchlist)
#   - Ad Hoc Engineering (unscheduled but legitimate activity)
#   - Vendor Activity (vendor maintenance not pre-registered)
#   - Batch/Process Change (operational pattern change)
#   - New Asset (asset not yet in OT_AssetRegister)
#   - Threshold Too Sensitive (detection threshold too low)
#   - Logic Gap (rule logic does not account for legitimate pattern)
#   - Environmental Change (network/process change affecting rule)
#   - Other
# Tuning Action Types:
#   - Watchlist Update (added maintenance window or asset entry)
#   - Exclusion Added (specific source/destination/pattern excluded)
#   - Threshold Adjusted (changed detection threshold)
#   - Logic Modified (changed rule logic)
#   - Baseline Updated (refreshed communication baseline)
#   - No Action (documented as known FP
#   - Rule Redesign (fundamental rule logic change required)
# Sample CSV:
EntryID
